FSFHap same-tag SNP filter (whichSnpsAreFromSameTag), faithful port

Source: R/RcppExports.R
fsfhap_same_tag_keep_cpp.Rd

Within a 64 bp window, consecutive SNPs whose presence-based R^2 to the window's anchor is >= min_rsq are treated as coming from the same GBS tag and dropped, keeping only the anchor; a SNP that is >= 64 bp away, or has R^2 < min_rsq (or NaN), becomes the next kept anchor. One chromosome per call (positions only; the chromosome-equality guard is implicit), markers sorted by position.

Usage

fsfhap_same_tag_keep_cpp(G, pos, major_is_ref, min_rsq)

Arguments

G

Integer matrix, taxa x sites, canonical g in {0,1,2,3}.

pos

Integer marker positions (bp), length = ncol(G), sorted ascending.

major_is_ref

Logical, length = ncol(G): TRUE if REF is the major allele at that site (so allele presence is defined per the site's own major/minor, as TASSEL's majorAllele/minorAllele do).

min_rsq

R^2 threshold for "same tag" (TASSEL default 0.8).

Value

Logical vector, length = ncol(G): TRUE = keep (a distinct tag).

On this page

Developed by Fausto Rodriguez Zapata.
